plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate high risk.
Trim income and broad index exposure to reduce overall risk.
Add high-quality tech name AAPL for stability.
Add strong risk/reward name COP for potential upside.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower target returns.
Expect a lower risk profile and smaller potential drawdowns.
conservative.read: This plan aims for ~1.5% per month by focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer. Expect lower potential drawdowns but also more modest growth.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs to a small allocation.
Reduce concentration in broad index ETFs.
Add quality tech names AAPL and AMD for growth.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth potential.
It aims for steady growth with moderate risk.
Expect moderate drawdowns and returns.
balanced.read: This plan targets ~2.5% per month with a risk-first growth approach. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate drawdowns and a balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETF SOXL.
Reduce concentration in broad index ETFs.
Increase position in strong performer CRWD.
Add inverse ETF SOXS for potential hedging.
Add quality tech name AAPL for growth.
This plan aggressively pursues growth with higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into leverage and concentration. It increases leveraged ETF exposure and adds to strong performers, accepting significantly higher potential drawdowns. Daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs can impact returns.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero.
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF QQQI.
Maintain a portion of broad index ETF SPY.
Add quality tech name AAPL for stability.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions.
Expect low drawdowns and reliable income, not high growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% per month by parking cash in income-generating ETFs and broad index funds. It minimizes leverage and high-beta single names to prioritize low drawdowns and reliable distributions. NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
